The paternal great-grandfather of the girl's paternal great-
grandfather.
5557
In the Chhadisht calculation, what is the 'Ninth Chhadisht'?
The paternal great-grandfather of the girl's maternal grandfather.
5558
Who is the 'Beeji Purush' of the 'Simbhuaal' Mool within the 
Parashar Gotra?
Rama
5559
Which Brahmin category is also referred to as 'Pubari-Paat'?
Shrotriya
5560
Which Brahmin category is also referred to as 'Pachhwari-Paat'?
All non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya, Panjibaddh, etc.).
5561
How many 'Chauvala' (classes) exist within the Shrotriya Panji?
8
5562
What are 'Laukik' within the context of the Panji system?
Specific titles or social sub-categories used primarily in the 
Shrotriya Panji records.
5563
Lineage: Ayanti Shrestha
Definition: The first and most prestigious category of primary 
Mools, including lineages like Khadaurya and Khauadya.
5564
Lineage: Madhyam Mool
Definition: The 'middle' category of Mools, such as Dighve, 
Panchobhe, and Budhabadhya.
5565
Which script was traditionally used to write the Panji records of 
Mithila?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
5566
What is 'Shakha Panji'?
A branching genealogical record that tracks individual families and 
their modern descendants.
5567
How many sages are associated with the 'Shandilya' Gotra Pravar?
3 (Shandilya, Asit, and Deval).
5568
How many sages are associated with the 'Vatsa' Gotra Pravar?
5 (Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnavan).
5569
Which historical scholar is cited as an early reference for 'Samooh 
Lekhya' (family records) in his work 'Tantra Vartika'?
Kumarila Bhatta.
5570
According to Kumarila Bhatta, why did Brahmins and Kshatriyas 
maintain 'Samooh Lekhya'?
To ensure they did not forget the traditions and names of their 
ancestors (Pita-Pitamah).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5571
The 'Vashistha' Gotra belongs to which Pravar category?
Tri-Pravar (Vashistha, Atri, and Sankriti).
5572
The 'Vishnuvriddha' Gotra belongs to which Pravar category?
Tri-Pravar (Vishnuvriddha, Kaitupuchh, and Trasadasyu).
5573
Which branch of Samaveda is followed by the 'Chhandogya' 
Brahmins of Mithila?
Kauthum Shakha.
5574
What is the primary difference in ceremonies between Chhandogya 
and Vajasaneyi Brahmins regarding 'Upanyana'?
Chhandogyas have four steps (Name, Tonsure, Thread, Return), 
while Vajasaneyis have five (Tonsure, Thread, Vedarambh, Return).
5575
How does the timing of the 'Uttara Vivah' differ for Chhandogya 
Brahmins?
It must take place after sunset after seeing the star Arundhati.
5576
Who was the 'Beeji Purush' of the 'Sarsab' Mool within the 
Shandilya Gotra?
Ratnapani
5577
Who was the 'Beeji Purush' of the 'Oini' Mool within the Kashyap 
Gotra?
Udyandev
5578
According to the 'Dooshan' records, what is an 'Agnyaat' marriage?
A marriage where the lineage or caste of the bride is unknown or 
undocumented.
5579
The record of ancestors found in the Panji is used not only for 
marriage but also for _____.
Pind-daan (funeral rites) and Ashauch (ritual impurity) calculations.
5580
Which Panji type specifically records the 'flaws' or social falls of a 
family lineage?
Dooshan Panji
5581
What is the 'Pan Panji'?
A record that includes the mention of 'Moolgrams' (ancestral 
villages), in use for about 500 years.
5582
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Jaywar' signify?
Lineages that were not formally 'Panjibaddh' or recorded in the 
primary Shrotriya/Yogya hierarchy.
5583
Which historical mathematician/astronomer is mentioned in the 
Panji lineage of the 'Mander' Mool?
Aryabhata
5584
According to the Panji lineage, Aryabhata was the son of _____.
Trivikram Bhatta
5585
The 'Panchobh' Mool belongs to which Gotra?
Savarna
5586
The 'Belonch' Mool belongs to which Gotra?
Bharadwaj
5587
Who is mentioned as the 'Guru' of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in the 
Panji records?
Ramapati Upadhyaya (from the 'Kimhe Tilauni' Mool).
5588
What happened to the Panji system among the Kshatriya 
community over time?
It largely disappeared, leading them to avoid marriage only within 
the same village (Dada-Matrik village avoidance).
5589
Who is the 'Beeji Purush' of the 'Simhashram' Mool?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
5590
The 'Kujauti' Moolgram belongs to which Gotra?
Katyayan
5591
If a girl and boy are 'Svijana', their offspring are traditionally 
categorized as _____.
Chandal
5592
What was the significance of the year 1248 Saka in Mithila's 
history?
It marks the traditional date of the 'Panji Prabandh' establishment by 
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
5593
What is 'Panji Prabandh'?
It is a systematic genealogical record-keeping system used in 
Mithila to verify caste purity and marriage eligibility.
5594
Who is credited with institutionalising the Panji system in 1326 AD 
(Saka 1248)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
5595
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (genealogist) appointed for Maithili 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
5596
Which historical incident prompted the formalisation of the Panji 
records?
Pandit Hari Nath Upadhyaya unknowingly married a close relative 
(his cousin's daughter) due to a lack of genealogical knowledge.
5597
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are traditionally recognised among 
Maithili Brahmins?
Twenty.
5598
What does the term 'Moola' signify in the Panji system?
The original ancestral village of a particular lineage.
5599
In the context of Panji, what is 'Utedh'?
The detailed introduction of 32 ancestral lines (paternal and 
maternal) used to determine marital eligibility.
5600
How many generations must be avoided on the father's side for a 
marriage to be considered valid?
Seven generations (modified to six in certain practices).
5601
How many generations must be avoided on the mother's side for 
marriage eligibility?
Five generations.
5602
What are the two primary Vedic branches followed by Maithili 
Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina/Vajasaneyi) and Samaveda 
(Kauthuma/Chhandogya).
5603
Why is intermarriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna 
Gotras?
Because they share the same ancestral rishis (common lineage).
5604
The document issued by a Panjikar certifying that a bride and 
groom are not related is called _____.
Siddhant Patra (or Asvajana Patra).
5605
What are the three hierarchical categories of Maithili Brahmins 
established by the Panji system?
Srotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha (or Jaywar).
5606
Which Panjikar was appointed by King Harisimhadeva for the 
Karna Kayastha community?
Shankardatt.
5607
The script traditionally used to write Panji manuscripts is _____.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5608
According to the Panji system, what is 'Chhadwith'?
A list of sixteen specific ancestral positions that must be checked to 
avoid 'Sapinda' (blood relation) marriages.
5609
Maithili Brahmins belong to which of the 'Pancha Gauda' groups?
Maithil.
5610
What is 'Moolagram'?
The specific village associated with the Beeji Purush of a clan, used 
as a primary identifier in genealogy.
5611
Which king introduced the 'Sakha Panji' to further categorise 
Brahmin lineages?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
5612
The first mention of 'group records' (Samuha Lekhya) to preserve 
lineage is found in which philosopher's work?
Kumarila Bhatta (in 'Tantra Varttika').
5613
What determines if a person is 'Panjibaddha'?
If their names and lineage are officially recorded in the Panji books.
5614
How does the 'adho-gami' principle affect social ranking in the 
Panji system?
It refers to the lowering of a family's status if they form marital 
alliances with lower-ranked lineages.
5615
Which month is considered ideal for drying Panji manuscripts to 
protect them from moisture and pests?
Bhadrapada (roughly 16th August to 15th September).
5616
How many main Mools (roots) were identified out of the 167 total?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five with the addition of the 'Khandam' 
branch).
5617
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of Srotriya records?
It is the specific Panji record or tradition maintained by Srotriya 
Brahmins.
5618
Which Vedic branch performs the 'Vedarambha' ritual as one of the 
four main Upanayana sanskars?
Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda).
5619
What is the significant difference in marriage rituals for 
Chhandogya followers compared to Vajasaneyis?
Chhandogya marriages are split into two parts (Purva and Uttara) 
and require sighting the Arundhati star after sunset.
5620
The 'Ayanti Shrestha' category includes which tier of Mools?
The first (highest) category of roots.
5621
In the Srotriya Panji, how many categories (Chauralla) are currently 
recognised?
Eight.
5622
The Panji system was also historically maintained for which group 
besides Brahmins and Kayasthas?
Kshatriyas (specifically the Gandhavariya Rajputs).
5623
What does the 'Siddhant' process involve?
A Panjikar verifies the 'Utedh' of both the bride and groom to 
ensure they are not related within prohibited degrees.
5624
In the Chhadwith calculation, who is the 'Pratham Chhadwith'?
The paternal great-great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamah).
5625
What is the primary role of a Panjikar in modern Mithila?
To act as a legal and religious authority for certifying the validity of 
marriages according to genealogical laws.
5626
Maithili Brahmins who contribute to the 'Sandilya' Gotra follow 
which Pravara?
Sandilya, Asita, and Devala.
5627
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Tri-pravara' sages Bharadwaja, 
Angirasa, and Barhaspatya?
Bharadwaja.
5628
The Panji records covering the period from 450 AD to 2009 AD are 
described by the author as a form of ancient _____.
Genome mapping.
5629
How did the geographical divide 'Vindhyachal' classify Indian 
Brahmins?
North of Vindhyachal are the 'Pancha Gauda' and south are the 
'Pancha Dravida'.
5630
What is 'Matripaksha' in the context of marriage verification?
The maternal lineage of the groom or bride.
5631
What happens if a girl appears as a 'Chhadwith' in the paternal 
lineage of the groom?
The marriage is prohibited (no 'Adhikar').
5632
Which Mool category is considered 'Madhyam' (Middle)?
The third category, including Mools like Dighwe, Belhaunche, and 
Ekahate.
5633
What scriptural authority is cited for the rule 'Matritah Panchamim 
Tyaktva Pitritah Saptamim Bhajet'?
Manusmriti (regarding skipping 5 maternal and 7 paternal 
generations).
5634
In the Panji, the 'kshana' or flaws recorded in 'Dushana Panji' can 
lead to a family being labelled as _____.
Dushit (flawed or degraded).
5635
Who was the 'Beeji Purush' for the Mandar Moola according to the 
records?
M.M. Trivenibhatt.
5636
The Panji system considers a person 'Svajana' if they share blood 
relations. What is the status of children from such a marriage?
They were traditionally viewed as 'Chandala' or social outcasts from 
the Aryan à¤®à¤°à¥à¤¯ à¤¾à¤¦à¤¾ (dignity).
5637
What is 'Pachhwari Paat'?
A classification referring to non-Srotriya Brahmins in the Panji 
system.
5638
How many total Panjis (genealogical registers) are mentioned in the 
source material?
185 (32 for Srotriyas and 153 for Yogya/Panjibaddha).
5639
Why is the preservation of Tirhuta script essential according to the 
Panjikars?
Because the entire historical corpus of Panji records is written in 
this script, and losing it would make the records inaccessible.
5640
What is the 'Tri-pravara' group of sages for the Kashyapa Gotra?
Kashyapa, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
5641
Which King's reign saw the start of documenting social 'kshana' 
(flaws) as a formal part of Panji?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
5642
In the Panji logic, if a 'Chhadwith' relation appears in the maternal 
side of the groom (Matripaksha), is the marriage allowed?
Yes, provided they are beyond the prohibited 5 generations.
5643
What was the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandha' established 
in medieval Mithila?
To maintain the purity of lineage (Jati Shuddhita) for Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5644
In the context of Maithil genealogy, what does the term 'Udegh' 
refer to?
The introduction of the lineage, requiring the identification of 32 
distinct ancestral branches.
5645
According to the Smriti laws cited in the text, how many 
generations must be avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
Seven generations.
5646
How many generations must be avoided on the maternal side for 
marriage according to traditional Dharmashastra rules?
Five generations.
5647
In which year (Christian era) was the Panji system officially 
systemised in Mithila?
$1326$ AD.
5648
The Panji system was established in the Shaka year _____.
$1248$
5649
Who was appointed as the first 'Panjikar' for Maithil Brahmins by 
King Harisimhadeva?
Ganaka Gunakar Jha.
5650
What is the total number of 'Gotras' identified among Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji records?
Twenty.
5651
How many 'Mools' (original village lineages) were initially 
documented in the Maithil Brahmin Panji?
$167$
5652
Out of the $167$ Mools, how many are considered the 'primary' or 
'main' Mools?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five with the addition of the Kharaur 
branch).
5653
The boundary dividing 'Panch-Gaur' and 'Panch-Dravid' Brahmins 
is the _____ mountain range.
Vindhyachal
5654
Which group of Brahmins does the Maithil community belong to in 
the broader classification of Indian Brahmins?
Panch-Gaur.
5655
What specific branch (shakha) of the Yajurveda is followed by 
Maithil Brahmins known as 'Vajasaneyi'?
Madhyandina Shakha.
5656
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Kauthum branch of the Samaveda 
are referred to as _____.
Chhandogya
5657
What is the primary difference in marriage rituals between 
'Chhandogya' and 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmins regarding timing?
Chhandogya weddings are divided into two parts and often involve 
viewing the star Arundhati after sunset.
5658
Marriage is strictly prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna 
Gotras because they share the same _____.
Ancestors (Pravaras)
5659
What is a 'Siddhant Patra' (or Asvajana Patra) in the Panji system?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
5660
Who were the appointed Panjikars for the Kayastha and Kshatriya 
communities during Harisimhadeva's reign?
Shankardutta (Kayastha) and Vijayadutta (Kshatriya).
5661
Which Maithil community is noted for having only one Gotra, the 
Kashyap Gotra?
Karn Kayastha.
5662
What does the term 'Beeji Purush' represent in a lineage record?
The earliest known progenitor or root ancestor of a specific family 
branch.
5663
The category of Maithil Brahmins who are not registered in the 
Panji records is called _____.
Jaywar
5664
How many 'Chhadhth' (ancestral positions) must be checked to 
determine marriage eligibility in the Panji system?
Sixteen.
5665
A marriage is prohibited if any of the bride's 16 'Chhadhth' 
ancestors appear in the groom's _____ side within prohibited 
degrees.
Paternal (Pitru)
5666
According to the rules, a groom can marry a bride who appears in 
his maternal side as long as she is at least in the _____ position.
Sixth
5667
Which historical event triggered the formalisation of the Panji 
system in the 14th century?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya inadvertently married a close relative 
due to a lack of genealogical records.
5668
What is the specific instruction for preserving Panji books 
regarding seasonal maintenance?
They should be dried in the sun when the Sun enters Leo (Sinha 
Rashi) to protect them from insects.
5669
What change did Maharaja Madhav Singh introduce to the Panji 
system in the 18th century?
The introduction of 'Sakha Panji' and the hierarchical classification 
of Shrotriya and other sub-categories.
5670
The hierarchical sub-groups 'Pubari' and 'Pachhwari' refer to the 
_____ and _____ sections of the Brahmin community respectively.
Shrotriya; non-Shrotriya
5671
How many 'Laukiks' (social status rankings) exist within the 
Shrotriya Panji category?
Thirty-two.
5672
In the rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami', what does 
'Saptami' refer to?
Avoiding the paternal lineage up to the seventh generation.
5673
What is the genealogical significance of the 'Mool Gram' in the 
records?
It identifies the ancestral village where the progenitor of the family 
first settled.
5674
Term: Asapinda
Definition: A person who does not share the same 'pinda' 
(body/prohibited lineage) and is therefore eligible for marriage.
5675
The 'Udegh Panji' is used primarily to trace the _____ of the 
ancestors.
Sapinda (blood relationship)
5676
Which philosopher's text, the 'Tantra-Vartika', is cited as the earliest 
reference to 'Samuha-lekhya' (group records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
5677
Under the modified rule by M.M. Mahesh Thakur, which paternal 
generation was deemed acceptable for marriage?
The seventh generation (meaning six generations must be passed).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5678
Name one of the ancestors included in the '16 Chhadhth' calculation 
for a bride.
The Paternal Great-Grandfather's Paternal Grandfather (Vriddha-
Prapitamah-Pitamah).
5679
What happens to the status of a lineage in the Panji if a member 
marries into a lower category?
The lineage status generally declines (Adhogami).
5680
The primary ancestors of the Shandilya Gotra are Shandilya, Asita, 
and _____.
Devala
5681
How many ancestors' identities are tracked in the full 'Udegh' of 32 
Mools?
Sixteen on the bride's side and sixteen on the groom's side.
5682
Which branch of knowledge is the 'Panji' system compared to in 
modern terminology within the text?
Genome Mapping.
5683
Which major Gotra is associated with the 'Oini' and 'Khauwal' 
Mools?
Kashyap.
5684
The 'Ayanti Shrestha' category belongs to which tier of Mool 
classification?
The first tier (First Class).
5685
What traditional warning is given to Panjikars regarding the 
physical handling of the Panji books?
To protect them from water, oil, and loose binding.
5686
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandha' system 
established in Mithila?
To maintain genealogical purity and regulate marriage eligibility 
among Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
5687
In the Panji system, what does 'Uderh' refer to?
The introduction and identification of seven generations of 
ancestors required for marriage verification.
5688
How many total 'Gotras' are identified within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the Panji records?
20
5689
Under the Panji system, what is the marriage exclusion rule 
regarding the father's side ('Pitri-paksha') for Shrotriyas?
Marriage is prohibited within seven generations of ancestors.
5690
Under the Panji system, what is the marriage exclusion rule 
regarding the mother's side ('Matri-paksha') for Shrotriyas?
Marriage is prohibited within five generations of ancestors.
5691
Which historical ruler of Mithila is credited with formalising the 
Panji Prabandha in the 14th century?
King Harisimhadeva.
5692
In what year (Shaka era and Gregorian) was the Panji system 
officially organised and documented under royal patronage?
Shaka 1248 (1326 AD).
5693
What term is used for the scholars appointed by royal decree to 
maintain genealogical records and verify marriage eligibility?
Panjikar.
5694
How many 'Main Mools' (Mukhya Mool) were initially identified 
within the 167 total original villages?
34 (later 35 after including the 'Khauam' branch of the 'Phandah' 
mool).
5695
What is the 'Siddhant Patra' (or Ashvajana Patra) in the context of 
Maithil marriage?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not prohibited kinsfolk.
5696
According to the Panji system, what happens to the offspring of a 
marriage that violates 'Sarpinda' rules?
The offspring are traditionally categorised as 'Chandal' and 
excluded from Aryan social à¤®à¤°à¥à¤¯ à¤¾à¤¦à¤¾ (dignity).
5697
Which philosopher's work 'Tantra Varttika' mentions 'Samuh 
Lekhya' as a precursor to formal genealogical records?
Kumarila Bhatta.
5698
What is the specific name for Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Madhyandina Shakha of the Yajurveda?
Vajasaneyi.
5699
What is the specific name for Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Kauthuma Shakha of the Samaveda?
Chandoga.
5700
In the 'Chandoga' (Samavedic) tradition, at what time is the 'Uttara 
Vivaha' performed?
After sunset, following the sighting of the star Arundhati.
5701
Maithil Brahmins are categorized as one of the five northern 
Brahmin groups known as the _____.
Pancha Gauda.
5702
According to the Panji Prabandha, how many 'Pravaras' define a 
'Tri-Pravara' lineage?
Three ancestors who were composers of Vedic hymns.
5703
Which three Gotras among Maithil Brahmins are classified as 
'Panch-Pravara' (having five sage ancestors)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharv.
5704
Who was the first Panjikar appointed specifically for the Karna 
Kayastha community?
Shankardatta.
5705
Which scholar was appointed by King Harisimhadeva to organise 
the Panji records for the Kshatriya community?
Vijaydatta.
5706
Who was the scholar from the 'Pandua' Mool appointed to oversee 
the Panji management for Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
5707
In the context of marriage eligibility, what does 'Chhatth' refer to?
The sixteen specific ancestral points (6th position in lineage) that 
must not overlap between the bride and groom.
5708
Which Maithil King initiated the 'Shakha Panji' system, leading to 
the hierarchical division of Shrotriyas and other classes?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
5709
The 'Laukik' refers to the specific Panji records associated with 
which social tier?
Shrotriya.
5710
What is the 'Dushan Panji' used for?
To record flaws or 'kshana' (deviations) in a lineage to warn future 
generations.
5711
Maha-Mahopadhyay Mahesh Thakur's modification to the Pitri-
paksha rule allowed marriage after _____ generations.
Six (the seventh generation is eligible).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5712
Why are the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' Gotras prohibited from 
intermarrying despite being different names?
Because they share the same 'Pravara' (ancestral sages).
5713
Concept: Siddhant
Definition: The final verification process where a Panjikar checks 
'Uderh' (genealogical charts) to grant marriage permission.
5714
How often should Panji books be dried to protect them from 
moisture and insects according to tradition?
Once a year when the Sun enters the Leo (Simha) constellation 
(approx. mid-August to mid-September).
5715
Which scholar's lineage includes Panjikar Modanand Jha in the 
18th generation?
Gunakar Jha.
5716
The script traditionally used for writing Panji records is _____.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
5717
How many 'Chhatth' (ancestral positions) are analyzed to determine 
the marriage eligibility of a girl?
16
5718
In marriage verification, if a 'Chhatth' ancestor appears in the 
groom's maternal side (Matri-paksha), is the marriage allowed?
Yes (it is prohibited only if it appears in the paternal side within 
specific limits).
5719
Identify the Gotra: Its sages are Shandilya, Asit, and Deval.
Shandilya.
5720
Identify the Gotra: Its sages are Atri, Vashistha, and Sankriti.
Vashistha.
5721
Which Maithil King's order led to the creation of 'Shakha Panjis' 
specifically for Shrotriyas in the 18th century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
5722
What are the two major divisions of the 10 Brahmin groups 
separated by the Vindhya mountains?
Pancha Gauda (North) and Pancha Dravida (South).
5723
The 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins consist of Utkala, Kanyakubja, 
Gauda, Maithil, and _____.
Saraswat.
5724
According to the Panji system, what constitutes the 'Battris Mool' 
(32 lineages) check?
The analysis of the 32 maternal and paternal lines of ancestors for 
both the bride and groom.
5725
The 'Vajasaneyi' wedding ceremony differs from the 'Chandoga' 
because it does not require _____.
A specific division into 'Purva-Vivaha' and 'Uttara-Vivaha' (it can 
happen day or night).
5726
What is the 'Adhikaar' document in Maithil marriage custom?
A list of potential grooms prepared by a Panjikar for a girl's father, 
based on genealogical eligibility.
5727
Which Brahmin tier is referred to as 'Pachhwari-paat'?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibadh).
5728
How many 'Chauvala' (categories) exist within the Shrotriya Panji?
8
5729
What is the primary technical challenge currently facing the 
preservation of Panji records?
The declining knowledge of the Tirhuta script among the general 
community and new scholars.
5730
According to the source, why did Brahmins in India eventually 
divide based on regional geography instead of just Veda branches?
Differences in climate, language, diet, dress, and difficulty of travel 
between regions.
5731
In the Panji system, 'Sarpinda' refers to ancestors sharing the same 
_____.
Pinda (ritual offerings/bloodline).
5732
What specific relationship is explicitly prohibited for a groom even 
if other rules are met?
The daughter of the groom's step-mother's brother.
5733
Who was the 'progenitor' (Beeji Purush) of the 'Vishwain' Mool 
(Simbari branch)?
Maha-Mahopadhyay Tritayan Bhatt.
5734
The 'Panji Prabandha' includes records of marriages with 
'Devadasis' to ensure that _____.
Future generations are aware of the lineage's history while 
remaining integrated into society.
5735
According to the source, the 'Samuh Lekhya' records began because 
high-born families did not want to forget their _____.
Ancestral traditions and the virtues/flaws of their lineage.
5736
What was the result of Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly 
marrying his cousin in the 13th century?
It acted as the catalyst for King Harisimhadeva to implement the 
mandatory Panji system.
5737
In the context of the Panji records, what does the term 'Anant' or 
'Agnyaat' signify in a family tree?
A point where the maternal or paternal ancestry is unknown or not 
recorded.
5738
What is the total number of 'Panjis' (books/records) managed by the 
Panjikars?
185 (32 Shrotriya and 153 Yogya/Panjibadh).
5739
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system 
introduced in the medieval period?
To maintain the caste purity of Brahmins, Kayasthas, and 
Kshatriyas through genealogical records.
5740
In the context of the Panji system, what does the term 'Utaidh' 
represent?
The detailed introduction of 32 ancestors spanning seven 
generations.
5741
Which historical event in the 14th century triggered the 
formalisation of the Panji records?
The accidental marriage of Pandit Harinath Upadhyay to a close 
relative due to lack of genealogical knowledge.
5742
Which ruler is credited with the institutionalisation of the Panji 
system in Mithila around 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
5743
According to the Panji management, what are the four distinct 
categories of Maithil Brahmins?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
5744
What defines the 'Jaywar' category of Maithil Brahmins?
Those who are not formally registered within the Panji records.
5745
In Maithil Brahmin marriage rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side to ensure eligibility?
Seven generations (modified to six in later practices).
5746
In Maithil Brahmin marriage rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the mother's side to ensure eligibility?
Five generations.
5747
How many primary or 'organized' Gotras exist among Maithil 
Brahmins that are most frequently cited?
Seven Gotras.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5748
The list of 20 Gotras includes Sandilya, Vatsa, Savarna, Katyayana, 
and _____.
Kashyapa
5749
What are 'Moolas' in the Panji system?
The original ancestral villages or roots of a family lineage.
5750
How many 'Moola Gramas' (root villages) were initially established 
as primary within the 167 total villages?
34 (later 35 with the addition of the Khanam branch).
5751
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Panch-pravar'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaja (some lists include Garga).
5752
What does the term 'Beeji Purusha' signify in a genealogical record? The earliest known common ancestor of a specific family lineage or 
'Moola'.
5753
The documentation of genealogical information by scholars in the 
7th century was first mentioned as 'Sammuha-lekhya' by which 
philosopher?
Kumarila Bhatta.
5754
In which philosophical text does Kumarila Bhatta describe 
'Sammuha-lekhya' as a tool for preserving lineage?
Tantra Vartika.
5755
What is the specific role of a 'Panjikar'?
An official registrar appointed to verify lineage and grant marriage 
eligibility.
5756
What is 'Sidhanta-patra'?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not 'Swajina' (prohibited relatives).
5757
What is the 'Chhadwith' (six-fold) check in Panji verification?
A set of 16 specific maternal and paternal relational points used to 
verify marriage eligibility.
5758
Which category of Maithil Brahmins follows the 'Kauthuma' branch 
of the Samaveda?
Chhandoga.
5759
Which category of Maithil Brahmins follows the 'Madhyandina' 
branch of the Yajurveda?
Vajasaneyi.
5760
What is a major procedural difference between Chhandoga and 
Vajasaneyi weddings regarding timing?
Chhandoga weddings must see the Arundhati star at sunset, while 
Vajasaneyi weddings can occur at any time.
5761
How many sub-categories or 'shakhis' of Shrotriya Brahmins were 
established based on hierarchy?
Eight categories.
5762
Under the Panji system, what does a 'Dushan Panji' record?
Defects or violations of tradition within a lineage to prevent future 
errors.
5763
Which scholar was appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Panji of Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
5764
Who was the first registrar appointed for the Panji of Karn-
Kayasthas?
Shankardutt.
5765
Which registrar was appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
oversee the Kshatriya lineage records?
Vijaydutt.
5766
How did the introduction of 'Shakha Panji' under Maharaja Madhav 
Singh change the social structure?
It introduced further sub-divisions and hierarchy, leading to social 
inequalities.
5767
What is 'Laukik' in the context of the Shrotriya Panji?
The specific hierarchical status or 'Pan' assigned to a family within 
the Shrotriya community.
5768
Identify the primary Gotra associated with the village of 'Khauauli'.
Katyayana.
5769
Which Gotra is linked to the Moola villages of 'Ekahara' and 'Pali'?
Kaundinya.
5770
In the Panji terminology, what is a 'Swajina' marriage?
An incestuous or prohibited marriage within the forbidden degrees 
of kinship.
5771
The ancestors of the Sandilya Gotra are Sandilya, Asita, and _____. Devala
5772
Which Gotra includes the sage ancestors Aurva, Chyavana, 
Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana?
Vatsa.
5773
The ancestors of the Kashyapa Gotra are Kashyapa, Avatsara, and 
_____.
Naidhruva
5774
Which Gotra shares the same Pravar as the Parashara Gotra?
Vashistha (specifically citing Shakti, Vashistha, and Parashara).
5775
What is the status of the 'Karn-Kayastha' Gotra according to the 
text?
They predominantly belong to a single Gotra, Kashyapa.
5776
What does 'Panji-Prabandh' imply regarding marriage and Shastric 
law?
A marriage is considered unscriptural (Ashastriya) if performed 
without a 'Sidhanta' certificate.
5777
According to the 'Chhadwith' calculation, the first check involves 
the bride's _____.
Great-great-grandfather (Vriddha-prapitamaha).
5778
In the context of the Panji, what are 'Paricheta'?
Collectors of genealogical data who travelled from village to village 
to record lineage.
5779
What script was historically used to write the Panji records in 
Mithila?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
5780
Why is it traditionally recommended to dry the Panji manuscripts 
during the month when the sun is in Leo (Singh Rashi)?
The heat is intense enough to preserve the manuscripts from 
moisture and pests.
5781
What is the 'Aswajanyapan Panji'?
A record of non-relatedness used for confirming eligibility.
5782
In the Panji tables, what does the term 'Agyat' (Unknown) signify?
A gap in the records where a maternal or paternal lineage could not 
be verified.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5783
What was the significance of the 1326 AD gathering in Mithila?
Scholars decided that lineage records should no longer be left to 
individual memory but must be officially recorded.
5784
Which Gotra is associated with the ancestors Angiras, Vashistha, 
and Brihaspati?
Gautama.
5785
The sages associated with the Bharadwaja Gotra are Bharadwaja, 
Angiras, and _____.
Barhaspatya
5786
Which type of Panji specifically records 'Kshayan' (defects) that 
have entered a lineage?
Dushan Panji.
5787
What is 'Adho-gami' in the Shrotriya classification?
The downward movement or loss of hierarchical status due to 
marriage into lower-ranked families.
5788
Which Gotra includes sages like Vishwavisnu and Angiras?
Katyayana.
5789
Which Gotra cites the ancestors Atri, Sankriti, and Vashistha?
Vashistha.
5790
Identify the Gotra of 'Brahmapura' Moola families if they are not 
Sandilya or Vatsa.
They can belong to Kashyapa, Alambuka, Gautama, or Bhargava.
5791
The term 'Panch-Gauda' refers to Brahmins from five northern 
regions, including Saraswat, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Utkal, and _____.
Maithil
5792
How does the Panji determine 'Sapinda' status for mothers in the 
Shrotriya category?
By checking the maternal side for five generations.
5793
What is 'Utaidh Panji' specifically used for?
To trace the Sapinda relationship and ensure non-relatedness before 
marriage.
5794
Which village is historically noted as the root for the 'Mandarya' 
lineage?
Mandi Grama.
5795
Who was the King of the Karnat Dynasty during the peak of the 
Panji organization?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
5796
In the Panji, the 'Beeji' ancestor of the Sandilya Gotra is often listed 
as _____.
Sandilya
5797
What does 'Srot-Plav' signify in the Panji categories?
A high-ranking status, often applied to the Shrotriya or noble 
lineages.
5798
The 'Tantra Vartika' text claims that high-born families preserve 
lineage to distinguish between _____ and _____.
Virtues (Guna) and defects (Dosha).
5799
How many ancestors are included in a full 'Utaidh' check?
32 ancestors.
5800
Which sage is the 'Beeji Purusha' for the village 'Sarasab' in the 
Panji?
Ratnapani.
5801
Which Gotra is associated with the ancestor 'Shandilya' and villages 
like 'Digho' and 'Mahua'?
Sandilya.
5802
The sages of the Vishnuriddha Gotra include Vishnuriddha, 
Kautipuccha, and _____.
Tradasya
5803
Which Gotra has 'Jatukarna', 'Angiras', and 'Bharadwaja' as its 
Pravar ancestors?
Jatukarna.
5804
What is 'Pari-Panji'?
A record of marital alliances that tracks the exchange between 
different Moolas.
5805
In the context of the Panji, what is the '1248 Shaka' date equivalent 
to in the Common Era?
1326 AD.
5806
The check for 'Chhadwith' points ensures the bride and groom are 
not related through their _____.
Great-great-grandmothers (Vriddha-prapitamahi).
5807
What is the primary reason for 'Panch-Dravida' and 'Panch-Gauda' 
divisions?
Geographic separation by the Vindhya mountains and regional 
cultural influences.
5808
What is the hierarchical relationship between Shrotriya and 
Panjibaddh Brahmins?
Shrotriyas are the highest-ranked, followed by Panjibaddh and 
Yogya.
5809
The Beeji Purusha for the Mandi Moola is _____.
Trivikram Bhatta
5810
Which Gotra is linked to the ancestors Atri, Garga, and Kaushika?
Kaushika.
5811
In which year (Shaka and AD) was the Panji Prabandh system 
officially formalised in Mithila?
It was formalised in 1326 AD, corresponding to Shaka 1248.
5812
Which King of the Karnat dynasty is credited with initiating the 
Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva is the King credited with its initiation.
5813
According to the source, what was the primary purpose of the Panji 
Prabandh?
It was created to maintain caste purity (Jati Shuddhata) for 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
5814
What does the term 'Udegh' signify in the Panji system?
It refers to the introduction of seven generations of ancestors 
required to verify marriage eligibility.
5815
How many 'Gotras' are traditionally identified within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
There are 20 identified Gotras.
5816
What is the meaning of 'Mula Gram' in the context of the Panji 
records?
It signifies the original ancestral village or place of residence for a 
family lineage.
5817
Which scholar was initially appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji?
The scholar Gunakar Jha was appointed for the Brahmin 
community.
5818
Name the scholar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to maintain 
records for the Karna Kayastha community.
Shankardatta was appointed to manage the Kayastha records.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5819
Which scholar was assigned the task of managing the Panji records 
for the Kshatriya community?
Vijayadatta was assigned the management of the Kshatriya records.
5820
The three hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins created by the 
Panji are Shrotriya, Yogya, and _____.
Panjibaddh.
5821
What term is used for those Brahmins who did not get registered in 
the initial Panji system?
They are referred to as 'Jaywar'.
5822
According to the Shrotriya marriage rules, how many generations 
must be avoided on the paternal side?
Seven generations must be avoided on the paternal side.
5823
How many generations must be avoided on the maternal side for a 
valid Shrotriya marriage?
Five generations must be avoided on the maternal side.
5824
In Panji terminology, what is a 'Siddhant Patra'?
It is a certificate of marriage eligibility issued by a Panjikar 
confirming the couple are not 'Svajana' (forbidden relatives).
5825
How many 'Mula' (original roots) were identified in the primary 20 
Gotras of Maithil Brahmins?
There were 167 primary Mulas identified.
5826
What are the four essential sacraments (Samskaras) for a 
'Chhandoga' (Samavedic) Maithil Brahmin?
They are Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
5827
How does the 'Vajasaneyi' (Yajurvedic) marriage timing differ from 
the Chhandoga tradition?
Vajasaneyi marriages can occur during the day or night, whereas 
Chhandoga marriages include a night ritual involving seeing the 
Arundhati star.
5828
The list of 20 Gotras includes Sandilya, Vatsa, Savarna, Kasyapa, 
and _____.
Parashara.
5829
What is the definition of 'Tripravara' in the Maithil Brahmin system? It refers to a lineage whose three ancestors were composers of 
Rigvedic hymns.
5830
Lineages where five ancestors composed Rigvedic hymns are 
categorised as _____.
Panchapravara.
5831
Term: Chhadisth
Definition: The 16 specific generational points or ancestors used to 
determine sapinda (forbidden) relations for marriage.
5832
What is the historical significance of the text 'Tantra Vartika' by 
Kumarila Bhatta to the Panji system?
It contains the earliest mention of 'Samuha Lekhya' (group records) 
used for preserving lineage purity.
5833
The highest category of Mulas, including Khadaurya and 
Khauadya, is known as _____.
Ayanti Shrestha.
5834
What was the specific social consequence for a child born from an 
'unauthorised' (Svajana) marriage?
The child was socially categorised as 'Chandal' and excluded from 
Aryan social status.
5835
Under Maharaja Madhav Singh in 1760 AD, what new type of 
Panji was introduced?
The 'Shakha Panji' was introduced, which detailed specific 
branches of lineages.
5836
What is the unique Gotra found within the Karna Kayastha 
community of Mithila?
Kasyapa is the only Gotra used by the Maithil Karna Kayasthas.
5837
The professional registrar who maintains and verifies these 
genealogical records is called a _____.
Panjikar.
5838
In the Panji system, what does 'Dushana Panji' record?
It records historical flaws or social 'blemishes' that occurred within 
a family lineage.
5839
Which month of the year is traditionally considered ideal for sun-
drying and preserving Panji manuscripts?
The period from mid-August to mid-September (when the sun is in 
Leo) is preferred for drying.
5840
According to the rules of 'Chhadisth', marriage is forbidden if any 
of the bride's 16 specific ancestors appear in the groom's _____.
Paternal lineage (Pitri-Paksha).
5841
Under what condition is a 'Chhadisth' ancestor overlap permitted 
for marriage?
It is permitted if the ancestor appears in the groom's maternal 
lineage (Matri-Paksha) rather than the paternal one.
5842
Which famous mathematician and astronomer's genealogy is 
recorded in the Panji as being from the Mandar Mula?
The genealogy of Aryabhatta is recorded.
5843
In the categorization of Brahmins, what is the 'Panch-Gauda' group? It refers to the five northern Brahmin groups: Utkala, Kanyakubja, 
Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
5844
What geographical feature traditionally divides the 'Panch-Gauda' 
from the 'Panch-Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
5845
According to the text, what is the status of Panji records among 
Maithil Kshatriyas today?
The Panji system among Kshatriyas has largely become extinct over 
time.
5846
What is the 'Paak' or 'Pani' in the context of Shrotriya Panji?
It refers to the relative social 'weight' or ranking within the 
hierarchical system.
5847
If a Panjikar issues a certificate for a marriage between forbidden 
relatives, what derogatory title was historically added to their name?
The title 'Taskar' (thief) was added to their name records.
5848
In the context of Samavedic rituals, 'Samadheni' refers to _____.
Vedic verses chanted while lighting the sacrificial fire.
5849
What does the term 'Asvajana Patra' signify?
It is an alternative name for the Siddhant Patra, confirming the 
couple are not related.
5850
Who was the 'Biji Purush' of the Mandar Mula, a prominent lineage 
in Mithila?
Mahamahopadhyaya Trayivikram Bhatta is identified as the Biji 
Purush.
5851
The 'Udegh' requirement for marriage involves checking _____ 
maternal and _____ paternal ancestors.
5 maternal and 7 paternal.
5852
Why is the preservation of Panji manuscripts specifically advised 
against oil (Taila)?
Oil is listed as a primary threat to the longevity and legibility of the 
physical manuscripts.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5853
In Panji records, what is recorded for a person categorized as 
'Devadasi'?
They are recorded as ancestors with 'unknown maternal lineage' 
(Agyat Matrika).
5854
Which scholar is noted as the Biji Purush for the Simhashram Mula? Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
5855
What are 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya records?
They are the specific social branchings and rankings within the 
Shrotriya tier.
5856
The 'Panji Prabandh' system is described as a precursor to modern 
_____ mapping.
Genome.
5857
Which Maithil King's court saw the first major categorization of 
Brahmins into the 'Shrotriya' tier?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
5858
What was the purpose of the 'Tipi' or 'Up-Tipi' records in the Panji 
system?
They served as auxiliary or summary notebooks for Panjikars to 
quickly reference lineages.
5859
What historical event led Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya to 
accidentally marry a cousin, triggering the formalization of Panji?
The lack of written genealogical records led to an accidental 
marriage within forbidden degrees of kinship.
5860
How many 'Chhadisths' are specifically derived from the bride's 
paternal grandmother's side?
Four (the 5th, 7th, 8th, and 15th Chhadisths).
5861
Which Mula is associated with the scholar Modanand Jha, who was 
awarded the title 'Panji Shastra Martand'?
The Mohaji (Maudgalya Gotra) lineage.
5862
The practice of reciting one's genealogy during a wedding is called 
_____.
Udegh Vachana.
5863
Why did the Panjikar system move from general oral memory to 
written manuscripts in 1326 AD?
To prevent accidental incestuous marriages due to the decline in 
individuals' personal genealogical knowledge.
5864
What is the primary role of the 'Biji' in a Panji entry?
It marks the starting point or root from which all subsequent 
branches of a family are tracked.
5865
According to the source, in which year (Saka and AD) was the 
Panji system formally organised under King Harisimhadeva?
Saka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
5866
The historical ruler credited with the formal implementation of the 
Panji system is Maharaja _____.
Harisimhadeva
5867
In the context of Panji, what does 'Udveh' refer to?
The introduction or genealogical identity of seven generations of 
ancestors required for marriage eligibility.
5868
Define the term 'Beeji Purusha' within the Panji system.
The earliest known founding ancestor of a specific lineage or clan.
5869
How many primary 'Gotras' are identified among Maithil Brahmins 
in the Panji records?
20
5870
What are the three main social categories of Maithil Brahmins 
established through the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha (also known as Jaibar).
5871
How many generations must be avoided for marriage eligibility on 
the paternal side according to the Shrotriya rule?
Seven generations.
5872
According to the general rule of 'Sapinda', how many generations 
must be avoided on the maternal side for marriage?
Five generations.
5873
The Panji system identifies a total of _____ 'Mulas' (original 
roots/villages) among the community.
167
5874
What does the term 'Mula Grama' signify in Maithil genealogy?
The original ancestral village from which a family lineage spread.
5875
Which two Vedas do the Maithil Brahmins primarily follow?
Samaveda (Kauthuma branch) and Yajurveda (Madhyandina 
branch).
5876
Maithil Brahmins following the Yajurveda are specifically referred 
to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
5877
Maithil Brahmins following the Samaveda are specifically referred 
to as _____.
Chhandogya
5878
What is the distinction between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Panch-pravara'?
Tri-pravara refers to lineages citing three ancestral Rishis, while 
Panch-pravara cites five.
5879
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Asvarjana Patra) in the Mithila 
marriage process?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not prohibited relations.
5880
The prohibition of marriage within the same Gotra is known as 
avoiding _____ marriage.
Sagotra
5881
What is 'Swarjana' in the context of Maithil marriage rules?
Marriage between individuals who are prohibited relatives, which 
the Panji system seeks to prevent.
5882
In the Panji system, how many 'Chadvitha' (genealogical 
checkpoints) must be checked to determine marriage eligibility?
16
5883
If a prohibited relation (Chadvitha) appears in the groom's paternal 
side, is the marriage permitted?
No, it is strictly prohibited.
5884
If a prohibited relation (Chadvitha) appears in the groom's maternal 
side beyond the fifth generation, is the marriage permitted?
Yes, it may be permitted under specific rule exemptions.
5885
Which Maharaja introduced the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) to 
further subdivide the social categories?
Maharaja Madhava Singh.
5886
The 'Dushana Panji' is a specific record used to document what?
Flaws or lapses in lineage purity within a specific family history.
5887
Identify the founding scholar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji.
Pandit Gunakar Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5888
Which scholar was appointed by King Harisimhadeva to manage 
the Karna Kayastha Panji?
Shankaradutta.
5889
Which scholar was appointed by King Harisimhadeva to manage 
the Kshatriya Panji?
Vijayadutta.
5890
According to the text, why did Brahmins divide based on regional 
geography into 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida'?
Due to differences in climate, language, diet, and local cultural 
influences over time.
5891
Maithil Brahmins belong to which of the two broad regional groups 
of Indian Brahmins?
Pancha Gauda
5892
What is 'Uteá¹›h' in the Panji system?
The detailed genealogical chart encompassing 32 ancestral roots 
(mulas) used for verification.
5893
The first 'Chadvitha' (checkpoint) for a bride involves checking the 
lineage of her _____.
Paternal Great-Great-Great Grandfather.
5894
Name one of the most prominent Mulas listed in the first category 
(Ayanthi Shrestha).
Khadaure or Khauadya.
5895
True or False: Marriage between the Vatsa Gotra and Savarna Gotra 
is traditionally prohibited.
True, because they share the same ancestors (Pravaras).
5896
What is the 'Laukik' in the Shrotriya Panji system?
Specific social status or 'honour' points that fluctuate based on 
marriage alliances.
5897
How many 'Chaupala' (categories) exist within the Shrotriya Panji?
8
5898
In the Sandilya Gotra, name two prominent Mulas.
Dighava and Sarisab.
5899
In the Kashyapa Gotra, name two prominent Mulas.
Oini and Mandar.
5900
The system of 'Panji Prabandh' is described as a precursor to 
modern _____ mapping.
Genome
5901
Which text by Kumarila Bhatta is cited as an early reference to 
'Samuha Lekhya' (group records)?
Tantra Varttika.
5902
What does 'Saman Gotra' mean in the context of marriage 
prohibition?
Belonging to the same paternal lineage or Gotra.
5903
What is the significance of the 35th Mula, 'Phandaha'?
It is an branch of the Khana Mula that attained high status in later 
periods.
5904
Which Gotra includes the Mulas 'Kujauli' and 'Tilauli'?
Katyayana
5905
The Vatsa Gotra includes which famous Mula from which many 
scholars originate?
Pali
5906
According to Manu and Yajnavalkya, a bride should not be a 
'Sapinda' of the _____.
Mother or Father.
5907
Define 'Pancha Gauda'.
The five northern Brahmin groups: Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, 
Maithil, and Saraswat.
5908
What was the immediate catalyst for King Harisimhadeva to 
formalise the Panji system in the 14th century?
A high-ranking scholar (Pandit Harinath Upadhyay) accidentally 
married his own cousin due to lack of records.
5909
What is the 'Pachhwari Paanth'?
The genealogical category for Brahmins other than the Shrotriya 
class.
5910
How many Panjis are dedicated to the Shrotriya class compared to 
the Yogya/Panjibaddha class?
32 Panjis for Shrotriya and 153 for Yogya/Panjibaddha.
5911
What is the total number of Panjis recorded in the system?
185
5912
Which Mula is associated with the Beeji Purusha 
'Mahamohopadhyaya Halayudha'?
Simhashrama
5913
The 'Shakha Panji' was specifically created to manage the lineages 
of which two groups?
Shrotriya and Panjibaddha.
5914
What information does the 'Mula Panji' primarily contain?
The original ancestral roots and primary lineages.
5915
What information does the 'Gotra Panji' primarily contain?
The lists of all Gotras and their associated ancient Mulas.
5916
Why is it important to protect Panji books from 'oil and loose 
binding' according to the traditional verse?
To ensure the physical longevity of the palm-leaf (Tala-patra) or 
paper records.
5917
In which month (Hindu calendar) were Panji books traditionally 
dried in the sun to prevent decay?
Bhadrapada (roughly Augustâ€“September) when the sun is in the 
Leo sign.
5918
The term for marriage eligibility criteria regarding 'maternal 
brother's children' is that they _____.
Must not be the same as the groom/bride.
5919
What does 'Anantarya' signify in the Panji categories?
The first or most excellent tier of original lineages (Ayanthi 
Shrestha).
5920
Identify the Gotra associated with the Mula 'Ekahara'.
Bharadwaja (also shared with Kaundinya).
5921
Which Gotra is associated with the Mula 'Uchiti'?
Vatsa
5922
The Mula 'Budhabal' is found in which two Gotras?
Vatsa and Kashyapa.
5923
According to the source, what is the 'Pancha-Dravida'?
The five southern Brahmin groups: Karnataka, Gurjar, Tailang, 
Dravida, and Maharashtra.
5924
What is the geographic boundary between 'Pancha Gauda' and 
'Pancha Dravida'?
The Vindhyachal mountains.
5925
What is the 'Adveá¹-h' in the context of marriage rights?
The verification of the 32 maternal and paternal ancestral lineages.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5926
True or False: The Panji records mention instances of marriages 
with individuals of unknown caste or from distant lands.
True, these are documented as exceptions in the 'Dushana' or 
'Apavad' sections.
5927
The scholar 'Vachaspati Mishra' is associated with which 
village/Mula in the Panji?
Palivash (Pali).
5928
Identify the Beeji Purusha of the 'Khadaure' Mula.
Ratnakara
5929
The 'Karna Kayasthas' primarily follow which single Gotra?
Kashyapa
5930
What is the significance of the year 1248 Saka in Maithil history?
It is the date of the formal 'Panji Prabandh' (genealogical regulation).
5931
Which Vedic branch's marriage involves seeing the 'Arundhati' star 
after sunset?
Chhandogya (Samavedi).
5932
True or False: The Vajasaneyi branch of Brahmins follows a 
different set of Upanayana (sacred thread) rituals than the 
Chhandogya branch.
True, the Chhandogya has four main Sanskaras while the 
Vajasaneyi has five.
5933
The total number of ancestral roots tracked to determine if a couple 
is 'Swarjana' is _____.
32
5934
What is the primary objective of the 'Panji Prabandha' system in 
Maithil society?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhi' (caste purity) by maintaining genealogical 
records to determine marriage eligibility.
5935
During which historical period was the Panji Prabandha system 
formally established for Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas?
The late medieval period, specifically the early 14th century.
5936
Who was the Karnat dynasty king responsible for the systematic 
implementation of Panji Prabandha in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
5937
The Panji system identifies how many total 'Gotras' (clans) among 
Maithil Brahmins?
Twenty.
5938
The formal introduction or 'Uderh' of an individual involves 
providing the lineage of how many ancestors?
Thirty-two.
5939
In the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy, what are the three primary 
categories created by the Panji classification?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
5940
What is the minimum number of generations to be skipped for 
marriage on the maternal side in the Shrotriya category?
Five generations.
5941
According to the general Panji rules, how many generations must 
be avoided on the paternal side for a valid marriage?
Seven generations.
5942
What is the total number of 'Moolas' (original lineages) documented 
within the Maithil Brahmin community?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
5943
The original village of residence that identifies a specific lineage is 
known as the _____.
Moolgram
5944
Term: Beeji Purusha
Definition: The earliest known progenitor or 'seed ancestor' of a 
specific lineage.
5945
How many main 'Moolas' were initially identified as the most 
prominent out of the 167 documented lineages?
Thirty-four (later adjusted to thirty-five with the inclusion of the 
Khanam branch).
5946
Which three Gotras are categorized under the 'Pancha-pravara' 
group?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaja.
5947
Brahmins following the 'Vajasaneyi' branch belong to which Veda?
Shukla Yajurveda.
5948
Brahmins following the 'Chhandogya' traditions are practitioners of 
which Veda?
Samaveda.
5949
Geographically, the 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins 
are divided by which natural boundary?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
5950
Which group within the 'Pancha Gauda' refers specifically to the 
Brahmins of the Mithila region?
Maithil Brahmins.
5951
Who was appointed by King Harisimhadeva as the first 'Panjikar' 
(registrar) for the Maithil Brahmins?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
5952
For the Karna Kayastha community, who was the first designated 
Panjikar?
Shankardatta.
5953
What script is traditionally used to record and preserve Panji 
genealogies?
Tirhuta (also known as Mithilakshar).
5954
The 'Dushana Panji' is used to record what specific information 
regarding a lineage?
Flaws or prohibited acts (faults) that might have occurred within the 
family history.
5955
Why is marriage generally prohibited between individuals of the 
'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' Gotras?
They share common ancestors, effectively making them of the same 
spiritual lineage.
5956
The term _____ refers to the sixteen specific branches of ancestors 
that must be checked to avoid 'Sapinda' relationships.
Chhadwih
5957
The 'First Chhadwih' in the marriage verification process 
specifically refers to whom?
The paternal great-grandfather of the bride's paternal great-
grandfather.
5958
Which social group in Mithila traditionally follows only the 
'Kashyapa' Gotra according to the text?
Karna Kayastha.
5959
How does the marriage ritual of Chhandogya Brahmins differ from 
Vajasaneyi regarding the stars?
Chhandogya marriages involve viewing the star Arundhati after 
sunset.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5960
Individuals whose names are officially recorded in the genealogical 
registers are called _____.
Panjibaddh
5961
The 'Laukik' system, used to track sub-lineages, is primarily 
associated with which category of Brahmins?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
5962
Which historical document is cited as the first mention of 'Samuha 
Lekhya' (collective records) in the 7th century?
Tantra Varttika by Kumarila Bhatta.
5963
Identify the Vedic branch followed by 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmins.
Madhyandina Shakha of the Yajurveda.
5964
Identify the Vedic branch followed by 'Chhandogya' Brahmins.
Kouthum Shakha of the Samaveda.
5965
According to 'Manusmriti', a woman who is not a 'Sapinda' of the 
father or mother is considered _____ for marriage.
Prashasta (recommended/eligible)
5966
In the Panji system, what does 'Anasvajan Patra' signify?
A document proving the absence of a prohibited blood relationship 
between two parties.
5967
Which specific ancestor does the 'Ninth Chhadwih' investigate for 
the bride?
The paternal great-grandfather of the bride's maternal grandfather.
5968
Why were Panjikar positions created as a state-authorised 
department in medieval Mithila?
To prevent accidental marriages between close relatives due to the 
loss of private family records.
5969
Which category of Brahmins is referred to as 'Pachhwari Paith' 
(Western Path) in contrast to Shrotriyas?
The remaining non-Shrotriya Panjibaddh Brahmins.
5970
What is the significance of 'Shakha Panji' in the Maithil system?
It is a branched registry that tracks the movement and expansion of 
lineages from the original Mool.
5971
The historical figure 'Vidyapati Thakur' belongs to which specific 
Mool and Gotra?
Bisaphi Mool and Kashyapa Gotra.
5972
How does the Panji system treat children of a marriage that violates 
'Sapinda' rules?
They were traditionally considered 'Chandala' (outcast) and 
excluded from the Aryan social order.
5973
Under which condition can a 'Chhadwih' (prohibited ancestor) be 
bypassed according to the text's logic on maternal/paternal sides?
A relationship that is prohibited on the paternal side might be 
allowed if it occurs on the maternal side.
5974
What was the role of 'Vijaydatta' in the initiation of the Panji 
Prabandha?
He was appointed as the first Panjikar for the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
community.
5975
The 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins include Saraswat, Kanyakubja, 
Gauda, Utkala, and _____.
Maithil
5976
The 'Pancha Dravida' group includes Gurjara, Maharashtra, 
Tailanga, Dravida, and _____.
Karnata
5977
Which unique ritual Samskara is performed by Vajasaneyi 
Brahmins but not typically by Chhandogya Brahmins?
Vedarmbha (commencement of Vedic study).
5978
In 'Uderh' terminology, what does the 'Sixteenth Chhadwih' 
represent?
The maternal grandfather of the maternal grandmother's maternal 
grandmother.
5979
What happened to the Panji system among the Kshatriyas of Mithila 
over time?
It largely became extinct, and they shifted to avoiding common 
village lineages for marriage.
5980
Which Mool is famously associated with the 'Ayachi' scholar 
lineage?
Sodarpur.
5981
How many 'Chaudala' (levels) are found in the Shrotriya Panji?
Eight.
5982
The Panji records were traditionally written on what material?
Palm leaves (Talapatra) or handmade paper (Basahapiti).
5983
What is the role of 'Maha-Mahesh Thakur' in the context of Panji 
evolution?
He simplified the paternal avoidance rule to skip only six 
generations, which was accepted by society.
5984
The phrase 'Jalat Raksha Tailat Raksha' in the Panji preservation 
verse advises protection against what?
Water and oil.
5985
Which specific months are recommended for drying and airing out 
the Panji manuscripts?
When the Sun is in the sign of Leo (roughly 16 August to 15 
September).
5986
The Panji Prabandha reflects which modern scientific concept 
through its ancient genealogical tracking?
Genome mapping or Genetic tracking.
5987
Who was the Karnata dynasty king of Mithila credited with 
formalising the Panji Prabandh system in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva
5988
What was the primary sociological purpose behind the creation of 
the Mithila Panji system?
To maintain caste purity and prevent prohibited marriages by 
recording lineages.
5989
How many distinct Gotras are identified among the Maithil 
Brahmin community according to the Panji records?
20
5990
According to the Smriti rules followed in the Panji, how many 
generations on the paternal side must be avoided for a valid 
marriage?
7 generations (later modified to 6 in some practices).
5991
In the context of Maithil marriage rules, how many generations on 
the maternal side must be renounced to ensure eligibility?
5 generations
5992
What term refers to the earliest known ancestor of a specific 'Mool' 
(lineage) in the Panji records?
Biji Purush
5993
Which social category represents the highest tier within the Maithil 
Brahmin hierarchy established by the Panji system?
Shrotriya
5994
Families who were formally recorded and registered in the 
genealogical books are known as _____.
Panjibaddh

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
5995
What is the name of the certificate issued by a Panjikar to certify 
that a bride and groom are eligible for marriage?
Siddhant Patra (or Aswajan Patra)
5996
Who was the scholar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
specifically manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha
5997
Which seven Gotras are considered the most populous and major 
lineages among Maithil Brahmins?
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyap, Savarna, Parashar, Bharadwaj, and 
Katyayan.
5998
Brahmins following the Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda are 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
5999
What is recorded in the 'Dushan Panji'?
Social defects, lapses in caste rules, or historical 'stigmas' within a 
lineage.
6000
M.M. Mahesh Thakur established the rule that the paternal line is 
acceptable for marriage starting from the _____ generation.
7th
6001
In the Chandoga (Samaveda) marriage tradition, what star must be 
sighted by the couple after sunset?
Arundhati
6002
Under what solar sign (Rashi) is it recommended to sun-dry the 
Panji palm-leaf manuscripts?
Simha (Leo)
6003
What was the significance of the 1326 AD gathering in Mithila 
regarding the Panji system?
It formalised the Panji system under state authority to resolve 
marriage eligibility disputes.
6004
The unique 'Brahmapura' Mool is found across how many different 
Gotras?
6
6005
Which category of 'Mool' includes the lineages of Khadaure, 
Khauade, and Budhabade?
Ayanti Shrestha (First Category)
6006
To which larger geographical classification of North Indian 
Brahmins do the Maithils belong?
Pancha-Gauda
6007
The Panji system claims to document lineages in Mithila dating 
back to approximately which year?
450 AD
6008
The five Pravar sages shared by the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras are 
Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and _____.
Apnuvana
6009
A Gotra that names three sage ancestors in the Rigveda is classified 
as _____.
Tri-Pravar
6010
Why is marriage prohibited between individuals belonging to the 
Savarna and Vatsa Gotras?
They share common ancestors and the same Pravar sages.
6011
Which mountain range traditionally separates the Pancha-Gauda 
Brahmins from the Pancha-Dravida Brahmins?
Vindhya Mountains
6012
What is the social consequence of a marriage deemed 'Swajana' 
(between prohibited relatives)?
The offspring are historically considered outcasts or 'Chandala'.
6013
In which script are traditional Mithila Panji records historically 
written?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar)
6014
How does the Panji system treat historical inter-caste marriages in a 
family tree?
They are recorded in the Dushan Panji to maintain genealogical 
honesty while allowing for eventual integration.
6015
The philosopher Bhavanath Misra, known as Ayanchi Misra, 
belonged to which famous Maithil Mool?
Sodarpur
6016
How many ancestors are formally checked in the 'Chhadwith' (six-
fold) verification process?
16 (representing specific paternal and maternal relations).
6017
What name is given to the eight categories of the Shrotriya Brahmin 
Panjis?
Chauwala
6018
The records for Kshatriya lineages in Mithila were initially 
entrusted to _____.
Vijayadatta
6019
Which scholar's 'Smritisar' inadvertently ignored the Sapinda rules, 
leading to the necessity of formal Panjis?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya
6020
The three Pravar sages for the Shandilya Gotra are Shandilya, Asita, 
and _____.
Devala
6021
In the Panji terminology, a person's identity is defined by their Mool 
and their _____.
Moolgram
6022
Which famous 7th-century philosopher mentioned 'Samuh Lekhya' 
as a way to protect noble lineages?
Kumarila Bhatta
6023
The Mools of Dighei, Sarisab, and Mahua are all associated with 
which Gotra?
Shandilya
6024
What is the purpose of the 'Aswajan Patra'?
To certify that the bride and groom do not share a common ancestor 
within prohibited degrees.
6025
What is the 'Utedh' in the Panji system?
A detailed genealogical introduction encompassing 32 branches of 
ancestors.
6026
Which Gotra includes the original village of Mandar and the lineage 
of Triayan Bhatta?
Kashyap
6027
How many primary 'Mools' (original village origins) were identified 
at the inception of the Panji system?
167
6028
The concept of 'Pravar' refers to ancestors who were the original 
composers of hymns in the _____.
Rigveda

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
6029
According to the Panji, the spiritual guru of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu 
belonged to which Maithil village origin?
Khaware-Tinauli
6030
What is the difference between 'Pubari' and 'Pachhwari' Brahmins 
in the Mithila hierarchy?
Pubari refers to the Shrotriya Brahmins, while Pachhwari refers to 
the lower tiers.
6031
The Panji records for the Karna Kayastha community were 
originally managed by which scholar?
Shankardatta
6032
The term 'Santati' (à¤¸à¤¨à¥ à¤¤à¤¤à¤¿) is used in genealogical 
records to denote _____.
A lineage or the continuous line of progeny from an ancestor.
6033
What is the primary function of a 'Mool' (à¤®à¥‚à¤²) in the Panji 
Prabandha?
It identifies the original ancestral root or home village of a specific 
Brahmin lineage.
6034
Which historical registry system is used to document the 
genealogies of Maithil Brahmins as shown in the source?
The Panji system (or Panji Prabandha).
6035
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Sarisab' (à¤¸à¤°à¤¿à¤¸à¤¬) 
village according to the Shandilya section?
Shandilya (à¤¶à¤¾à¤£à¥ à¤¡à¤¿à¤²à¥ à¤¯ ).
6036
According to the text, the 'Sodarpura' (à¤¸à¥‹à¤¦à¤°à¤ªà¥ à¤°) village 
is categorized under which Gotra?
Shandilya (à¤¶à¤¾à¤£à¥ à¤¡à¤¿à¤²à¥ à¤¯ ).
6037
The abbreviation 'M.M.U.' found before names like Jivanatha and 
Bhavanatha stands for _____.
Mahamahopadhyaya (a title for a great scholar).
6038
To which Gotra does the village 'Oini' (à¤“à¤‡à¤¨à¤¿) belong, as 
per the genealogical classifications?
Kashyapa (à¤•à¤¾à¤¶à¥ à¤¯ à¤ª).
6039
Under which Gotra are the 'Pali' (à¤ªà¤²à¥ à¤¿à¥€) and 'Hariamb' 
(à¤¿à¤°à¤¿à¤…à¤®à¥â€à¤ª à¤¬) lineages classified?
Vatsa (à¤µà¤¤à¥ à¤¸).
6040
Which ancestor is linked to the 'Satainh' (à¤¸à¤¤à¥ˆà¤¢à¤¼) branch 
within the Sarisab village records?
Bhavadityapatya (offspring of Bhavaditya).
6041
The 'Khandwala' (à¤–à¤£à¥ à¤¡à¤¬à¤¿à¤¾) village is traditionally 
linked with which Gotra?
Shandilya (à¤¶à¤¾à¤£à¥ à¤¡à¤¿à¤²à¥ à¤¯ ).
6042
Which Gotra includes the villages 'Niun' (à¤¨à¤¿à¤‰à¤¨) and 
'Sakuri' (à¤¸à¤•à¥à¤¿à¥€)?
Parashara (à¤ªà¤¿à¤¾à¤¶à¤¿).
6043
The 'Oinwar' (Oinisa) branch is stated to be prominent within the 
_____ Gotra.
Kashyapa (à¤•à¤¾à¤¶à¥ à¤¯ à¤ª).
6044
In the records, the lineage of 'Ratnakara' (à¤°à¤¤à¥ à¤¨à¤¾à¤•à¤°) is 
associated with which specific village branch?
Khandwala (à¤–à¤£à¥ à¤¡à¤¬à¤¿à¤¾).
6045
The village 'Jajuar' (à¤œà¤œà¥ à¤†à¤¿) is a notable branch 
belonging to the _____ Gotra.
Vatsa (à¤µà¤¤à¥ à¤¸).
6046
Which scholarly title is associated with the individual 'Gonu' 
(à¤—à¥‹à¤¨à¥‚) in the Kampila village records?
Mahamahopadhyaya (M.M.U.).
6047
